rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when bounds are concrete or visible
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings of existing outputs or auxiliary variables
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers when available; otherwise rely on the authoritative AADL model structure
Place assumptions in component type annexes and guarantees in implementation annexes when appropriate
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Align helper declaration types with the expression types for valid comparisons
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Comparison expressions are bool; integer literals and arithmetic are int; real literals and arithmetic are real
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not encode AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants
